items.correct: Early learning of norms and values, primarily within the family.
items.misconception: The most important socialisation, happening in school.
items.why: Students confuse 'primary' with primary school. Primary = first/early (family); secondary = later (school, peers, media). The terms describe ORDER, not importance or location.

items.q: What is a moral panic according to Cohen?
items.correct: Disproportionate media reaction creating exaggerated public concern about a group or behaviour.
items.misconception: Genuine widespread fear about a real, serious threat.
items.why: Students miss the 'disproportionate' element. The concept is critical of media amplification - it's about REACTION exceeding the threat, not about whether the underlying issue is real.

items.q: How does radical feminism differ from liberal feminism?
items.correct: Radical feminists see patriarchy as the primary system of oppression requiring fundamental change.
items.misconception: They are essentially the same with minor differences.
items.random_wrong: Radical feminists oppose all women's rights.
items.why: Students treat feminisms as one bloc. Liberal feminists seek reform within existing systems; radical feminists see patriarchy as inherent to those systems and demand structural transformation.
The answer examiners want is: Faludi. “Greer” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.
Correct answer: Crenshaw. “Parsons” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In which year did the #MeToo movement go viral globally?
items.why: Exact answer: 2017. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
